what_it_does: Defines a small shared CanvasHeader React component that renders a canvas header bar: a title (h1), an optional subtitle (p), and an optional actions slot. Attaches it to window.CanvasHeader.
observations: Subtitle and actions are conditionally rendered only when provided
Uses class names only (dsa-canvas-header / -title-block / -title / -sub / -actions) — no inline styles
Exposed via window.CanvasHeader rather than ES export
conventions: Follows the project's window-global component pattern
Pure class-based styling (defers all look to the design-system CSS / tokens) — no raw px or hex, on-system
notes: A tiny presentational header (title + optional subtitle + actions slot) reused across canvases via window.CanvasHeader.
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the comment calls it a shared canvas header and that is exactly what it is.
intention_signals: Provides one consistent header shell every canvas/editor reuses, so titles, subtitles, and action toolbars look identical everywhere without duplication.
contribution: Contributes the shared canvas header layout primitive — title + optional subtitle + actions slot — that the builder canvases compose.
summary_for_embedding: A small shared React CanvasHeader component rendering a title, optional subtitle, and an optional actions slot, exposed on window.CanvasHeader.
intention_for_embedding: To give every canvas/editor one consistent, class-styled header shell with a title, subtitle, and actions slot, avoiding duplicated header markup.
